faults.title: Overcurrent
faults.desc: Check motor cables, mechanical load blockage, or short acceleration time.

faults.title: Earth Fault
faults.desc: Check motor cable insulation and motor terminals against ground.

faults.title: DC Overvoltage
faults.desc: Check input voltage, lengthen deceleration time, or use braking resistor.

faults.title: Output Phase Loss
faults.desc: Check motor cable connections at drive terminals (U2, V2, W2) and motor.

faults.title: Heatsink Overtemp
faults.desc: Check cooling fans, panel filter cleanliness, and Ambient temperature.

faults.title: Safe Torque Off (STO)
faults.desc: Check STO safety circuit (XSTO) and ensure both channels are active.

faults.title: Motor Stall
faults.desc: Check for locked rotor or if current limit parameters are too low.

faults.title: Motor Overload
faults.desc: Check for motor overload or motor thermal parameter settings.

faults.title: Input Phase Loss
faults.desc: Check input fuses and stability of main power supply (L1, L2, L3).

faults.title: Comm Loss
faults.desc: Check connection between Control Panel (CDPI) or PC Tool and drive.

faults.title: Ground Fault
faults.desc: Check for current leakage in motor cables or internal motor parts.

faults.title: Short Circuit
faults.desc: Check for short circuits in motor cables or inverter modules.

faults.title: DC Charging Error
faults.desc: Check input voltage or damage to internal charging circuit.

faults.title: Control Panel Loss
faults.desc: Check panel mounting or panel cable if mounted on the panel door.

faults.title: External Fault
faults.desc: Check external signals connected to Digital Input (usually external trip relay).

faults.title: Wiring or Earth Fault
faults.desc: Check for incorrect input and motor cable connections (i.e. input power cable connected to drive motor connection).

faults.title: Cooling Fan Fault
faults.desc: Main or auxiliary cooling fan speed drops below the limit. Check fan wiring and for debris blocking the fan.

faults.title: External Temperature 1
faults.desc: External temperature sensor (PTC/Pt100) connected to drive detects overtemperature in motor/machinery.

faults.title: EFB Comm Loss
faults.desc: Embedded fieldbus (Modbus RTU) communication lost. Check PLC cable, termination resistors, and baud rate.

faults.title: FBA A Comm Loss
faults.desc: Communication lost between drive and Fieldbus Adapter A (e.g., FENA-11 Profinet). Check ethernet cable or PLC heartbeat.

faults.title: External Fault 1
faults.desc: Fault triggered by a programmable external signal via Digital Input. Check the physical switch or interlock state.

faults.title: Extension I/O Configuration Failure
faults.desc: The installed I/O extension module (e.g. FIO-01) does not match the drive parameter configuration.

faults.title: ID Run Fault
faults.desc: Motor identification run failed. Ensure motor nameplate data (99.xx parameters) is strictly correct before ID Run.

faults.title: Control Board Temperature
faults.desc: The control unit (ZCU/BCU) is critically hot. Check if the control board fan is running or if ambient is exceeding 55°C.

faults.title: DC Link Undervoltage
faults.desc: Intermediate circuit DC voltage is too low. Check input phase supply drop or main contactor tripping unexpectedly.

faults.title: Brake Control Fault (Crane)
faults.desc: [`ACS880 +N5050`] **Mechanical brake logic failure**. Check brake feedback acknowledge signal, contactors, and brake resistor.

faults.title: Brake Slip (Crane)
faults.desc: [`ACS880 +N5050`] **Motor shaft movement detected** while the mechanical brake is commanded closed. Check brake pad wear/tightness.

faults.title: Torque Proving (Crane)
faults.desc: [`ACS880 +N5050`] Motor **failed to build sufficient electrical holding torque** against the closed brake before opening. Check motor data.

faults.title: Speed Match (Crane)
faults.desc: [`ACS880 +N5050`] The motor actual speed deviates too much from the speed reference. Usually caused by **overloaded hoist** or **encoder loss**.

faults.title: Snag Load (Crane)
faults.desc: [`ACS880 +N5050`] Hook or load **caught on a mechanical obstruction** during hoisting up, instantly jerking the torque. Inspect the load path.
